metadata.greeting: This weekend in London is packed with exciting activities for your little one! From exploring the free exhibits at the Natural History Museum to interactive storytelling at the Southbank Centre, there's something for everyone to enjoy. With scattered clouds and a temperature of 13°C on Sunday, it's a great weekend to venture outdoors for some fun!

activities.title: Library Club at the Hackney Showroom
activities.description: Reading corner and diverse book-pairing service.
activities.whyItWorks: Free, drop-in, and caters to a wide age range, offering books suitable for a 3-year-old.

transport.fromHackney: Bus or Overground to Hackney Downs or Hackney Central
transport.tip: Check term times before visiting.

activities.title: Sound Explorers at Young V&A
activities.description: Interactive sound-based activities for young children.
activities.whyItWorks: Sensory experience, perfect for toddlers, and free!

transport.tip: Arrive early to secure a spot.

activities.title: Young V&A
activities.description: Interactive galleries designed for children, including play areas and exhibits.
activities.whyItWorks: The museum is specifically designed for children with hands-on activities.

transport.tip: Check out the Mini Museum area for under 2s.

activities.title: Mudlarks Family Gallery
activities.description: Interactive gallery with soft play and activities exploring London's history.
activities.whyItWorks: Soft play is ideal for toddlers, and it's a free activity.

transport.tip: Visit during off-peak hours to avoid crowds.

activities.title: Biodiversity Playground
activities.description: Wooden playground with a pond theme designed for toddlers.
activities.whyItWorks: Designed specifically for toddlers with safe and engaging play equipment.

transport.tip: Combine with a walk around the park.

activities.title: The Waterworks
activities.description: Jets and sprays for children to enjoy on warm days.
activities.whyItWorks: Fun water play, perfect for a warm day.

transport.tip: Bring a towel and change of clothes.

activities.title: Museum of the Home
activities.description: Explore the history of homes and domestic life.
activities.whyItWorks: While not specifically for toddlers, it offers a sensory experience, and it's free.

transport.tip: Check for family-friendly events.

activities.title: Inside Aardman: Wallace & Gromit and Friends
activities.description: Explore how the iconic Aardman characters are brought to life.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive exhibits and well-known characters will engage a 3-year-old.

transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: Natural History Museum
activities.description: Explore dinosaurs, mammals, and other natural wonders.
activities.whyItWorks: Dinosaurs are always a hit with toddlers, and there's plenty of space to move around.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria Line to Green Park, then the Piccadilly Line to South Kensington
transport.tip: Visit during off-peak hours to avoid crowds.

activities.title: Science Museum
activities.description: Interactive exhibits for kids to discover how the world works.
activities.whyItWorks: The Garden area in the basement is specifically designed for young children.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria Line to Green Park, then the Piccadilly Line to South Kensington
transport.tip: Book a free admission ticket in advance

activities.title: Golders Hill Park Zoo
activities.description: Small zoo with rare species of birds and animals.
activities.whyItWorks: Small and manageable for toddlers, and free entry.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Northern Line to Golders Green
transport.tip: Combine with a picnic in the park.

activities.title: Diana Memorial Playground
activities.description: Playground with a Peter Pan-inspired pirate ship and sensory trail.
activities.whyItWorks: Large, engaging playground with a variety of activities.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria Line to Oxford Circus, then the Central Line to Notting Hill Gate
transport.tip: Can get busy, arrive early.

activities.title: Hampstead Heath Ponds & Playgrounds
activities.description: Large park with ponds, playgrounds, and open spaces.
activities.whyItWorks: Plenty of space for running around, playgrounds, and nature to explore.

transport.tip: Perfect for a picnic.

activities.title: Cutty Sark
activities.description: Explore the historic tea clipper ship.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive exhibits and a chance to explore a real ship.

transport.tip: Check for family activities.

activities.title: National Maritime Museum
activities.description: Discover maritime history with child-friendly activities.
activities.whyItWorks: The AHOY! Children's gallery is perfect for under 7s.

transport.tip: Check out the All Hands Children's Gallery.

activities.title: Horniman Museum and Gardens
activities.description: Natural history displays, musical instruments, and gardens.
activities.whyItWorks: Manageable size, gardens to explore, and free entry.

transport.tip: Check out the animal enclosure.

activities.title: Lindt Gold Bunny Hunt
activities.description: Explore the gardens and discover Lindt Gold Bunny statues.
activities.whyItWorks: Fun Easter-themed activity in a beautiful setting, with a chocolate treat at the end.

transport.fromHackney: Train from Liverpool Street to Hampton Court
transport.tip: Allow 90 minutes to complete the trail.

activities.title: Zoonormous Egg Hunt
activities.description: Search for giant Easter eggs hidden around the zoo.
activities.whyItWorks: Combines a zoo visit with an Easter egg hunt.

transport.fromHackney: Tube to Regent's Park or Camden Town
transport.tip: Pick up a trail card at the entrance.

activities.title: Easter Egg Trail: the Spring Sprites
activities.price: Paid, in addition to paid admission
activities.description: Follow the trail, search for the eggs and discover the places where the Sprites live. Along the way, complete small tasks and playful challenges to make a special potion to help awaken the Sprites.
activities.whyItWorks: Outdoor activity combines nature with interactive fun

transport.tip: Pre-booking tickets recommended

activities.title: The Very Hungry Caterpillar Trail
activities.description: Discover a variety of illustrated animals nestled amongst the blooming habitats of the reserve, identify the letter on each one and complete the secret word in the activity booklet.
activities.whyItWorks: Engaging and educational nature trail suitable for young children

transport.tip: Check for special events or workshops.

activities.title: Easter Quest at Eltham Palace and Gardens
activities.price: Paid, in addition to paid admission
activities.description: Embark on a fun-filled adventure quest around the house and gardens. Follow the Easter trail, hunt for eggs and answer questions along the way to earn a delicious chocolate reward.
activities.whyItWorks: Combines exploration with a reward suitable for toddlers

transport.tip: Check for special events or workshops.

activities.title: Tower Bridge - Easter family activities
activities.dates: Tuesday 31 March, Tuesday 7 April and Saturday 11 April 2026
activities.description: Discover phenakistoscopes, the original animations that came before cartoons and GIFs. Create your own phenakistoscope of another Victorian icon – Tower Bridge!
activities.whyItWorks: Craft activities, especially suited to slightly older toddlers (3+) with good fine motor skills.

transport.fromHackney: Tube to London Bridge or Tower Hill
transport.tip: Pre-booking required.

activities.title: Alice in Wonderland
activities.description: A theatrical adaptation of the classic tale.
activities.whyItWorks: A classic story brought to life on stage.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Hammersmith and City Line to Hammersmith
transport.tip: Check age suitability before booking.

activities.title: Kew Gardens – Sounds of Blossom
activities.price: Prices from: ~£15 adult (children cheaper / under-4s free)
activities.description: Explore the sounds of spring at Kew Gardens.
activities.whyItWorks: Outdoor exploration with sensory elements.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: District Line to Kew Gardens
transport.tip: Check for family-friendly events.
